title: hypericum elongatum subsp. racemosum
intro: Hypericum elongatum subsp. racemosum, also known as the St. John's Wort of the Caucasus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort genus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with Hypericum elongatum subsp. racemosum include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is typically harvested during the summer months when the flowers are in full bloom.
